sessions.summary: Explored and summarized the WhatsApp chatbot architecture, including webhook entrypoint, agent handler, session/context storage, prompt definitions, action/tool files, and conversation history flow.
Created and persisted the SDD tasks artifact for chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset after retrieving proposal/spec/design and inspecting the affected Convex files.

Implemented the chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset apply scope with stronger pre-LLM filtering, shared deterministic off-topic fallback, transport-owned request completion notices, and conversation rotation that preserves closed history. Verified touched files with targeted ESLint; automated tests remain pending because no test runner exists.
Completed verify phase for chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset, saved verify report, and identified missing runtime tests plus a closureApplied contract mismatch.
Se creó y persistió la propuesta SDD para reestructurar servicio/chat con ramas condicionales, snapshot de dirección, adjuntos múltiples y pago/comprobante con validación admin.
Cerré el batch crítico del apply: sync de chat determinístico en sesión, branches editables en config UI y detalle/admin operativo con snapshot/comprobante/validación.

Cerré 5.1, 5.2 y 6.3 del apply SDD con kill switch global/backend+frontend, normalización segura de requests históricos, cobertura de integración del flujo migrado y actualización de artifacts en Engram.

# Exploración: Estructura del Proyecto Pere Tantico

## 1. ¿Qué es Pere Tantico?

**Pere Tantico** es un sistema de gestión de solicitudes/servicios para logística de entrega a domicilio (tipo Rappi/Glovo). Es una aplicación React/Vite con:

- **Frontend**: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + TailwindCSS + Radix UI
- **Backend**: Convex (base de datos + auth + API)
- **Auth**: Convex Auth (autenticación)
- **AI**: Integración con Google AI y OpenAI (posiblemente para chatbot)
- **Estado**: Zustand (estado global) + TanStack Query
- **UI**: shadcn/ui + Framer Motion
- **WhatsApp Bot**: Integración con YCloud para mensajería

## 2. Módulos/Features Existentes

### Estructura de `src/features/`:

| Feature | Propósito |
|---------|-----------|
| `auth/` | Login, logout, reset password, guards de rol |
| `home/` | Dashboard principal - Gestión de solicitudes |
| `client/` | Gestión de clientes/solicitantes |
| `distributors/` | CRUD de repartidores con filtros |
| `reports/` | Panel de control, estadísticas, exportación Excel |
| `costs/` | Módulo de costos y pagos |
| `users/` | Gestión de usuarios del sistema |
| `config/` | Configuraciones del sistema |
| `inbox/` | Chat de WhatsApp con clientes (bot) |

### Estructura de `src/components/`:

| Carpeta | Contenido |
|---------|-----------|
| `common/` | Componentes compartidos (AlertModal, SearchInput, Paginator, etc.) |
| `layout/` | DashboardLayout |
| `navigation/` | AppSidebar (menú lateral) |
| `ui/` | Componentes shadcn/ui (Button, Card, Dialog, etc.) |

## 3. Roles de Usuario

| Rol | Código | Descripción |
|-----|--------|-------------|
| **Administrador** | `admin`, `administrador`, `superadmin` | Acceso completo a todas las secciones |
| **Repartidor** | `distributor`, `repartidor` | Solo ve la página principal (gestión de solicitudes) |

### Roles verificados en código:
- `app-sidebar.tsx` - Filtra navegación según rol
- `role-guard.tsx` - Protege rutas basadas en roles
- `App.tsx` - Excluye distribuidores de rutas de admin

## 4. Principales Flujos/Pantallas

### Pantallas identificadas:

1. **Login** (`/iniciar-sesion`)
   - Formulario de autenticación
   - Reset password (`/restablecer-contraseña`)
   - Nueva contraseña (`/nueva-contraseña`)

2. **Dashboard Principal** (`/`)
   - **Admin**: Gestión de solicitudes con filtros
   - **Repartidor**: Vista simplificada (DistributorDashboard)

3. **Clientes** (`/clientes`)
   - Gestión de clientes/solicitantes
   - CRUD de clientes

4. **Repartidores** (`/repartidores`)
   - CRUD de repartidores
   - Filtros: zona cobertura, estado disponibilidad, tipo transporte, estado pago

5. **Costos** (`/costos`)
   - Módulo de costos y pagos
   - Historial de pagos generados

6. **Reportes** (`/reportes`)
   - Panel de control con estadísticas
   - Grillas de solicitudes y repartidores
   - Exportación a Excel

7. **Configuración** (`/configuraciones`)
   - Configuraciones del sistema

8. **Usuarios** (`/usuarios`)
   - Gestión de usuarios del sistema

9. **Inbox** (`/inbox`)
   - Chat de WhatsApp con clientes
   - Control del bot (pausar/reactivar)

## 5. Navegación (Sidebar)

```typescript
// En src/constants/index.ts
navMain = [
  { title: "Solicitudes", url: "/", icon: Home },
  { title: "Clientes", url: "/clientes", icon: Users },
  { title: "Repartidores", url: "/repartidores", icon: Bike },
  { title: "Costos", url: "/costos", icon: DollarSign },
  { title: "Reportes", url: "/reportes", icon: BarChart3 },
  { title: "Configuraciones", url: "/configuraciones", icon: Settings },
  { title: "Inbox", url: "/inbox", icon: MessageSquare },
]
```

## 6. Estados de Solicitud

- `En análisis`
- `En trámite`
- `EnProceso`
- `Finalizada`
- `Atendida`
- `Incompleta`
- `Rechazada`

## 7. Recomendaciones para Manual de Usuario

### Estructura sugerida:
1. **Introducción**
   - Qué es Pere Tantico
   - Requisitos del sistema
   
2. **Primeros Pasos**
   - Login/logout
   - Navegación básica
   
3. **Guía por Rol**
   - Manual para Administradores
   - Manual para Repartidores
   
4. **Módulos Detallados**
   - Gestión de Solicitudes
   - Gestión de Clientes
   - Gestión de Repartidores
   - Costos y Pagos
   - Reportes y Estadísticas
   - Configuración
   - Inbox (Chat WhatsApp)
   
5. **Referencia Rápida**
   - Estados de solicitud
   - Glosario de términos

### Formato sugerido:
- Documento HTML/Markdown con screenshots
- Videos tutoriales por módulo
- FAQ de problemas comunes

observations.title: sdd/manual-usuario/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: User Manual for Pere Tantico

## Intent

Create a comprehensive user manual for Pere Tantico — a service request/logistics management application — to onboard new users, reduce support queries, and document system functionality for Admin and Repartidor roles.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Full documentation of all 8 modules: Home (solicitudes), Clientes, Repartidores, Reports, Costs, Users, Config, Inbox (WhatsApp)
- Role-specific guides: Admin (full access) and Repartidor (limited view)
- Markdown-based modular documentation with clear section hierarchy
- Quick-start guide for each role

### Out of Scope
- Developer/API documentation
- Video tutorials or interactive walkthroughs
- Localization (Spanish-only for v1)
- Printed/PDF version

## Approach

1. **Modular Markdown Structure**: Each module gets its own `.md` file under `/docs/manual/` with role-based sub-sections
2. **Screenshot Integration**: Placeholder tags for screenshots (e.g., `![alt](path/to/screenshot.png)`) — screenshots to be added manually
3. **GitHub Pages Hosting**: Deploy via GitHub Actions to `https://{org}.github.io/peretantico/manual/`
4. **Version Alignment**: Manual version tracks app version (v1.0, v1.1, etc.)

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `docs/manual/` | New | User manual Markdown files |
| `.github/workflows/manual.yml` | New | GitHub Pages deploy workflow |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Screenshots become outdated with UI changes | High | Add "Last verified: [date]" to each section; review on major releases |
| Manual falls out of sync with app features | Medium | CI checks for manual presence on release; add to definition of done |

## Rollback Plan

Delete `docs/manual/` directory and disable GitHub Pages deployment workflow. No database or code changes required.

## Dependencies

- GitHub repository with GitHub Pages enabled
- Write access for docs contributor or maintainer role

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 8 modules documented with at least basic descriptions
- [ ] Both roles (Admin, Repartidor) have quick-start sections
- [ ] Manual deployed and accessible via GitHub Pages
- [ ] Screenshot placeholders in at least key modules

observations.title: sdd/manual-usuario/spec
observations.content:
# User Manual Specification — Pere Tantico
## Delta Spec: Reorganización del Manual de Usuario

## Purpose

Redefine requirements, scenarios, and structure for the Pere Tantico user manual with a new **teaching/learning flow** that guides users from system setup to daily operations.

---

## NEW Content Order (Teaching Flow)

The manual **MUST** follow this progressive order to ensure logical learning:

---

### 1. CONFIGURACIÓN DEL SISTEMA (`/configuraciones`)

**Purpose**: Base configuration that enables all other modules.

**File**: `config.md`

#### Requirements

**C001: System Configuration Module**

The system **MUST** allow Admin users to configure:

- **Servicios Disponibles**: Define list of service types (e.g., delivery, pickup, installation)
- **Fechas Especiales**: Mark holidays and non-working days that affect scheduling
- **Categorías**: Create service categories for classification
- **Zonas de Cobertura**: Define geographic zones for service availability

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: Define Available Services**

- GIVEN the user is in Configuración > Servicios
- WHEN the Admin adds a new service with name, description, and base price
- THEN the service appears in dropdown menus when creating solicitudes
- AND is available for all future requests

**Scenario: Mark Holiday**

- GIVEN the user is in Configuración > Fechas Especiales
- WHEN the Admin adds a date as "Feriado" with a name
- THEN that date is excluded from delivery scheduling
- AND a notification appears on affected pending solicitudes

---

### 2. REPARTIDORES (`/repartidores`)

**Purpose**: Manage delivery personnel who fulfill service requests.

**File**: `distributors.md`

#### Requirements

**R001: Repartidor CRUD**

The system **MUST** allow Admin to:

- **Crear Repartidor**: Add new delivery person with name, phone, vehicle type
- **Actualizar Datos**: Modify repartidor information
- **Generar Contraseñas**: Auto-generate temporary password for new repartidor
- **Cambiar Contraseñas**: Admin can reset password for any repartidor

**R002: Repartidor Status**

The system **MUST** track repartidor status:

- **Activo**: Can receive new assignments
- **Inactivo**: Cannot log in; existing assignments remain

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: Create Repartidor with Auto-Password**

- GIVEN the user is in Repartidores > Agregar
- WHEN the Admin fills: name, phone, vehicle type, and clicks "Crear"
- THEN the system generates a random 8-character password
- AND displays it ONCE for Admin to share with repartidor
- AND the repartidor account is created with "Activo" status

**Scenario: Reset Repartidor Password**

- GIVEN the Admin is viewing a repartidor's detail page
- WHEN the Admin clicks "Cambiar Contraseña" and confirms
- THEN a new password is generated
- AND the old password is immediately invalidated

---

### 3. GESTIÓN DE SOLICITUDES (`/` - Home)

**Purpose**: Core workflow for service requests, building on Config and Repartidores.

**File**: `home.md`

#### Requirements

**G001: Create Solicitud**

The system **MUST** allow users to:

- Select service type (from configured services)
- Assign client information
- Select delivery zone
- Assign a repartidor (from active repartidores)
- Set scheduled date/time (respecting holidays)

**G002: Solicitud States**

The system **MUST** track these statuses:

| State | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Pendiente | Created, awaiting repartidor |
| Asignada | Repartidor confirmed |
| En Progreso | Service being performed |
| Completada | Service finished |
| Cancelada | Request cancelled |

**G003: Assign Repartidor**

The system **SHOULD** validate that the selected repartidor:

- Has "Activo" status
- Is available on the requested date
- Has no conflicting assignments

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: Create Solicitud**

- GIVEN the user has configured services and has active repartidores
- WHEN the user clicks "Nueva Solicitud" and fills: client, service, zone, repartidor, date
- THEN the solicitud is created with "Pendiente" status
- AND the repartidor receives notification (if enabled)

**Scenario: Change Solicitud State**

- GIVEN the user is viewing a solicitud detail
- WHEN the Admin or Repartidor updates the status via dropdown
- THEN the status changes and timestamp is recorded
- AND the activity log is updated

---

### 4. MANUAL DEL REPARTIDOR (Sección Especial)

**Purpose**: Dedicated guide for Repartidor role users.

**File**: `repartidor-guide.md` (new section)

#### Requirements

**MR001: Repartidor Authentication**

The system **MUST** allow repartidores to:

- **Iniciar Sesión**: Access with phone number and password
- **Recibir Notificaciones**: Get alerts for new assignments
- **Cerrar Sesión**: Log out securely

**MR002: Solicitud Management (Repartidor View)**

The system **MUST** allow repartidores to:

- **Ver Solicitudes Asignadas**: List of all assigned service requests
- **Actualizar Estado**: Change status from assigned to in-progress to completed
- **Cargar Evidencia**: Upload photos as proof of service completion

**MR003: Photo Upload**

The system **MUST** accept:

- JPG and PNG formats
- Maximum 5 photos per solicitud
- Photos timestamped at upload time

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: Repartidor Login**

- GIVEN the repartidor has credentials (phone + password)
- WHEN they access the login page and enter credentials
- THEN they see a limited dashboard with: Mis Solicitudes, Inbox
- AND admin modules are hidden

**Scenario: Upload Service Evidence**

- GIVEN the repartidor is viewing a solicitud detail
- WHEN they click "Agregar Evidencia" and select photos
- THEN photos are uploaded and stored with the solicitud
- AND the status automatically suggests "Completada" option

---

### 5. MÓDULO DE COSTOS ADMIN (`/costos`)

**Purpose**: Financial management and payment liquidation for Admin.

**File**: `costs.md`

#### Requirements

**CO001: Pricing Logic**

The system **MUST** document (in manual) and implement:

- Base price per service type (from Config)
- Zone surcharges (if applicable)
- Special date adjustments (holiday rates)

**CO002: Payment Grouping**

The system **MUST** group solicitudes for payment:

- By Repartidor
- By Date Range
- By Status (completed only)

**CO003: Generate Liquidations**

The system **MUST** allow Admin to:

- Select date range and repartidor
- Calculate total based on completed solicitudes
- Generate liquidation document (PDF or printable)
- Mark liquidation as "Pagada" to close period

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: View Cost Summary**

- GIVEN the user is in Costos module
- WHEN the Admin selects a repartidor and date range
- THEN the system shows: total services, amount per service, grand total
- AND only completed solicitudes are included

**Scenario: Generate Payment Liquidation**

- GIVEN the Admin is viewing a cost summary
- WHEN they click "Generar Liquidación"
- THEN a PDF is generated with: repartidor name, period, itemized services, total
- AND the liquidation is saved with status "Pendiente de Pago"

---

### 6. REPORTES Y ESTADÍSTICAS (`/reportes`)

**Purpose**: Analytics and reporting for business insights.

**File**: `reports.md`

#### Requirements

**RP001: Solicitud Statistics**

The system **MUST** display:

- Total solicitudes by status (pie chart)
- Solicitudes over time (line chart)
- Average completion time

**RP002: Repartidor Statistics**

The system **MUST** display per repartidor:

- Total completed services
- Average rating (if implemented)
- On-time completion rate

**RP003: Payment Reports**

The system **MUST** show:

- Total payments made per period
- Outstanding liquidations
- Export to Excel functionality

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: View Dashboard Statistics**

- GIVEN the user is in Reportes module
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN charts display: solicitudes by status, top repartidores, monthly trends
- AND data is filterable by date range

**Scenario: Export Report**

- GIVEN the user is viewing a report
- WHEN they click "Exportar a Excel"
- THEN a `.xlsx` file downloads with current view data
- AND filename format is: `reporte_{tipo}_{YYYYMMDD}.xlsx`

---

### 7. INBOX (`/inbox`)

**Purpose**: WhatsApp chatbot integration and client communication.

**File**: `inbox.md`

#### Requirements

**I001: Chatbot Behavior**

The system **MUST** include an automated chatbot that:

- Responds to common queries (status, schedule)
- Guides clients through simple workflows
- Escalates complex issues to human operators

**I002: Bot Control**

The system **MUST** allow Admin to:

- **Desactivar Bot**: Temporarily disable auto-responses
- **Configure Responses**: Set custom replies for keywords
- **View Chat History**: Access all conversations

**I003: Direct Response**

The system **MUST** allow users to:

- Reply to client messages directly from the platform
- Send via WhatsApp integration
- Track message delivery status

#### Scenarios

**Scenario: Deactivate Chatbot**

- GIVEN the Admin is in Inbox > Configuración
- WHEN they toggle "Bot Activo" to OFF
- THEN all incoming messages receive no auto-response
- AND clients see a "En espera de respuesta" message

**Scenario: Reply from Platform**

- GIVEN the Admin is viewing a client chat thread
- WHEN they type a message and click "Enviar"
- THEN the message is sent via WhatsApp API
- AND appears in chat with "Enviado ✓" status

---

## File Structure (Updated)

```
docs/manual/
├── index.md                    ← Main index with nav (updated order)
├── 1-configuracion.md         ← NEW: System Config
├── 2-repartidores.md          ← NEW: Distributor Mgmt
├── 3-solicitudes.md           ← NEW: Service Requests (from home)
├── 4-manual-repartidor.md     ← NEW: Repartidor Guide
├── 5-costos.md                ← Admin Cost Module
├── 6-reportes.md              ← Reports & Stats
├── 7-inbox.md                 ← Chatbot Integration
└── _old/                       ← Legacy files (for reference)
    ├── auth.md
    ├── home.md
    ├── clients.md
    ├── users.md
```

---

## Version Synchronization Rules

1. `index.md` **MUST** contain `Version: X.Y.Z` matching app version
2. `CHANGELOG` section **MUST** be appended on each release
3. Screenshots **MUST** have `Last verified: YYYY-MM-DD` timestamp
4. **NEW**: Each section file **MUST** have a "Tiempo estimado de lectura" note (e.g., "⏱ ~5 min")

---

## Content Structure Summary

| Order | Section | File | Key Content |
|-------|---------|------|-------------|
| 1 | Configuración del Sistema | 1-configuracion.md | Services, holidays, zones |
| 2 | Repartidores | 2-repartidores.md | CRUD, passwords, status |
| 3 | Gestión de Solicitudes | 3-solicitudes.md | Create, assign, states |
| 4 | Manual del Repartidor | 4-manual-repartidor.md | Login, photos, assignments |
| 5 | Módulo de Costos | 5-costos.md | Pricing logic, liquidations |
| 6 | Reportes | 6-reportes.md | Stats, charts, exports |
| 7 | Inbox | 7-inbox.md | Chatbot, WhatsApp, responses |

**Total sections**: 7 (consolidated from 8+ modules)

---

## Removed Requirements (Legacy)

- **Clientes module** (merged conceptually into Solicitudes workflow)
- **Users module** (admin-only, lower priority for learning flow)
- **Auth module** (moved into Quick-Start section, not standalone)

observations.title: sdd/manual-usuario/design (updated)
observations.content:
# Design: Pere Tantico User Manual Documentation System

## Technical Approach

Create a lightweight documentation system for the Spanish-language user manual using **GitHub Pages + Markdown** (via VitePress). This provides:
- Zero additional build complexity (Vite already in stack)
- Fast local preview for developers
- Git-based versioning and review workflow
- Easy screenshot embedding via relative paths
- Built-in PDF export via VitePress's print plugin
- Video embed support for walkthroughs

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Static Site Generator

**Choice**: VitePress
**Alternatives considered**: MkDocs, Docusaurus, Next.js docs, pure Markdown + GitHub Pages
**Rationale**: VitePress integrates seamlessly with the existing Vite build system, offers Vue-based theming for custom layouts, provides faster dev server than MkDocs, and has built-in PDF export capabilities. Spanish documentation benefits from VitePress's i18n support for future English version.

### Decision: Documentation Location

**Choice**: `client/docs/`
**Alternatives considered**: Separate `/docs/` repo, `/docs/` at repo root
**Rationale**: Keeping docs in `client/` enables:
- Single PR for feature + docs changes
- Shared CI/CD pipeline
- Easy local preview with `vitepress dev`
- Screenshots can reference relative UI paths

### Decision: Screenshot Strategy

**Choice**: Local storage in `docs/public/screenshots/` — **single mode (no dark/light variants)**
**Alternatives considered**: Cloud storage (imgix), external CDN, base64 embedded, dual-mode screenshots
**Rationale**: Local storage ensures offline access, no external dependencies, and version control. Single mode chosen per user decision — only one screenshot per view is needed, reducing maintenance burden.

### Decision: Video Strategy

**Choice**: Local `.mp4` files in `docs/public/videos/` with optional YouTube/Vimeo embeds
**Alternatives considered**: External video hosting only, no video support
**Rationale**: Key workflows (solicitudes creation, costos liquidation) benefit from video walkthroughs. Local storage provides offline access; external embeds provide scalability if videos grow large.

### Decision: PDF Export

**Choice**: VitePress `@pdf` export plugin (vitepress-plugin-pdf or similar)
**Alternatives considered**: Puppeteer/headless Chrome in CI, third-party PDF services
**Rationale**: VitePress ecosystem has mature PDF plugins that render the entire site to a single downloadable PDF. No external services needed.

## File Structure

```
client/docs/
├── .vitepress/
│   ├── config.mts           # VitePress configuration with PDF + video support
│   └── theme/
│       ├── index.ts         # Custom theme overrides
│       └── styles/
│           └── custom.css   # Brand colors, fonts
├── index.md                 # Main landing page
├── public/
│   ├── screenshots/        # Organized by module (single mode)
│   │   ├── configuracion/
│   │   ├── repartidores/
│   │   ├── solicitudes/
│   │   ├── repartidor-guide/
│   │   ├── costos/
│   │   ├── reportes/
│   │   └── inbox/
│   └── videos/             # Video walkthroughs (optional)
│       ├── solicitudes-crear.mp4
│       └── costos-liquidacion.mp4
└── chapters/
    ├── 1-introduccion.md
    ├── 2-configuracion.md
    ├── 3-repartidores.md
    ├── 4-solicitudes.md
    ├── 5-manual-repartidor.md
    ├── 6-costos.md
    ├── 7-reportes.md
    └── 8-inbox.md
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    VitePress Build                          │
│  Markdown Files ──→ VuePress/Vite ──→ Static HTML/CSS      │
│        │                                      │              │
│        ├── docs/public/screenshots/* ←─── Screenshots       │
│        └── docs/public/videos/* ←─────── Local Videos       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
           │                              │
           ▼                              ▼
┌─────────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│   GitHub Pages      │    │     PDF Export (via plugin)    │
│   (HTML site)       │    │     Manual completo.pdf         │
└─────────────────────┘    └─────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Video Embed Strategy

| Video Type | Hosting | Embed Method |
|------------|---------|---------------|
| Key workflow demos | Local (`/public/videos/`) | `<video>` tag or file download link |
| Long-form tutorials | YouTube/Vimeo | `<iframe>` embed in markdown |
| Quick tips | Local | Inline `<video controls>` |

**Videos to include**:
1. `solicitudes-crear.mp4` — Creating a service request (5-7 min)
2. `costos-liquidacion.mp4` — Generating payment liquidations (3-5 min)

## PDF Export Configuration

```typescript
// .vitepress/config.mts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitepress'
import pdf from 'vitepress-plugin-pdf' // or similar plugin

export default defineConfig({
  // ... base config
  vite: {
    plugins: [pdf({
      outputPath: 'dist/manual.pdf',
      include: ['/chapters/**/*'],
      exclude: ['/api/**']
    })]
  }
})
```

## Interfaces / Contracts

### Screenshot Naming Convention

Format: `{feature}-{action}-{state}.{ext}`

Examples:
- `config-servicios-agregar.png`
- `solicitudes-estado-completada.png`
- `repartidor-evidencia-upload.png`

### Video Naming Convention

Format: `{workflow}-{action}.{ext}`

Examples:
- `solicitudes-crear.mp4`
- `solicitudes-asignar.mp4`
- `costos-liquidacion-generar.mp4`

### Markdown Frontmatter

```yaml
---
title: Configuración del Sistema
description: Guía para configurar servicios, zonas y fechas especiales
lastVerified: 2026-03-18
readingTime: 5 min
hasVideo: true
videoFile: ../public/videos/configuracion-inicial.mp4
---
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Build | VitePress builds without errors | CI: `vitepress build` on PR |
| Links | All internal links resolve | CI: `vitepress build --check` or link checker |
| Screenshots | Files referenced exist | CI: script checks `public/screenshots/` coverage |
| Videos | Video files exist for referenced embeds | CI: script validates `hasVideo: true` entries have files |
| PDF | PDF generates without errors | CI: generate PDF on release tag |
| Spelling | Spanish content spelling | CI: `cspell` with `es` dictionary |

## CI/CD Pipeline

```yaml
# .github/workflows/docs.yml
name: Deploy Documentation
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['client/docs/**']
  pull_request:
    paths: ['client/docs/**']

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - run: cd client && npm ci
      - run: npm run docs:build
      - name: Generate PDF
        run: npm run docs:pdf
      - uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: client/docs/.vitepress/dist

  pdf-release:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: cd client && npm ci
      - run: npm run docs:pdf
      - name: Upload PDF artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: manual-pdf
          path: client/docs/.vitepress/dist/manual.pdf
```

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. Documentation is net-new content.

**Phased rollout**:
1. Create `docs/` structure and VitePress config
2. Write chapters 1-3 (core setup flow) — **with screenshots**
3. Add video walkthroughs for solicitudes and costos
4. Configure PDF export plugin
5. Deploy to staging GitHub Pages
6. Add remaining chapters
7. Final review and production deploy
8. Generate and release v1.0 PDF on first stable tag

## Open Questions

- [x] ~~Should screenshots use dark/light mode variants?~~ **DECIDED: No, single mode only**
- [x] ~~Include video walkthroughs for complex flows?~~ **DECIDED: Yes, for solicitudes and costos workflows**
- [x] ~~Support for PDF export of manual?~~ **DECIDED: Yes, add to scope**
- [ ] Capture screenshots in CI or manual process?
- [ ] Video hosting: local only, or YouTube/Vimeo for scalability?

**What**: Mapped the WhatsApp chatbot architecture for Pere Tantico Tequendo, including webhook entrypoint, agent orchestration, state tables, prompt sources, and tool definitions.
**Why**: The user requested an exploration of handler files, session/context management, prompts, actions like listServices/getServiceFields, and conversation history flow.

observations.title: Session summary: peretantico-client
observations.content:
## Goal
Explore the WhatsApp chatbot architecture for Pere Tantico Tequendo and identify handlers, session/context storage, prompts, tools, and conversation history flow.

## Instructions
- Investigate real code only.
- Focus on WhatsApp/chatbot/agent/conversation/session/context/prompt/instructions files.
- Return architecture summary with file paths and key components.

## Discoveries
- The runtime entrypoint is an HTTP webhook in `convex/http.ts` that schedules `convex/ycloudBot.ts` for async processing.
- The bot uses three separate persistence layers: `botSessions`/`botApplicants` for business/session state, `ycloudMessages` for raw message history, and `conversations` for inbox summaries.
- AI history is also maintained separately through Convex Agent threads; `threadId` is mirrored into both `conversations` and `botSessions`.
- The active prompt is `TANTICO_AGENT_PROMPT` in `convex/system/ai/constants.ts`; `convex/system/tanticoPrompt.ts` appears to be an older/alternate Gemini rewrite prompt and is not referenced by the WhatsApp flow.
- `botSessions` has fields like `serviceId`, `fieldIds`, `currentFieldIndex`, and `data`, but current code mostly initializes/resets them instead of advancing them directly in `ycloudBot.ts`.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Identified main handlers: webhook in `convex/http.ts`, processor in `convex/ycloudBot.ts`, agent definition in `convex/system/ai/agents/tanticoAgent.ts`.
- ✅ Located session/context storage in `convex/whatsappBotState.ts`, `convex/conversationState.ts`, `convex/ycloudState.ts`, and `convex/schema.ts`.
- ✅ Located prompt/instruction sources in `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` and noted legacy prompt files in `convex/system/tanticoPrompt.ts` and `.trae/OLD_PROMTS.md`.
- ✅ Located tool/action definitions in `convex/system/ai/tools/`, including `listServices.ts`, `getServiceFields.ts`, `validateServiceField.ts`, `createApplicantProfile.ts`, `createRequest.ts`, and `getRequestStatus.ts`.
- ✅ Traced conversation history handling for bot replies, inbox UI, and reset/handoff behavior.

## Next Steps
- Clarify whether `botSessions.serviceId/currentFieldIndex/data` are intended for future explicit orchestration or should be removed if thread/tool state is sufficient.
- If needed, inspect Convex Agent component internals to document exactly how thread messages are persisted beyond the app-level `threadId` references.

## Relevant Files
- `convex/http.ts` — YCloud webhook parsing, verification, and scheduling.
- `convex/ycloudBot.ts` — Main inbound processor, agent invocation, reset logic, off-topic guardrails, and outbound sending.
- `convex/system/ai/agents/tanticoAgent.ts` — Agent registration with model, instructions, and toolset.
- `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` — Main operational prompt for Tantico.
- `convex/whatsappBotState.ts` — Applicant/session persistence for WhatsApp chats.
- `convex/conversationState.ts` — Inbox conversation summary and thread linkage.
- `convex/ycloudState.ts` — Raw message log, dedupe, locks, and handoff mute state.
- `convex/system/ai/tools/listServices.ts` — Service listing tool.
- `convex/system/ai/tools/getServiceFields.ts` — Dynamic field discovery tool.
- `convex/system/ai/tools/validateServiceField.ts` — Field validation and file storage tool.
- `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts` — Request creation and post-create thread/session reset.
- `convex/schema.ts` — Table definitions for all chatbot-related state.

observations.title: Investigated Tantico session and thread reset gaps
observations.content:
**What**: Investigated the WhatsApp bot flow and found that botSessions do not actually track progression through a request; serviceId, fieldIds, and currentFieldIndex are only cleared/reset, never populated, while the LLM thread is reused until manual reset or createRequest success.
**Why**: The user asked for root-cause analysis of off-topic filtering and indefinite context growth in Tantico.
**Where**: convex/ycloudBot.ts, convex/whatsappBotState.ts, convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts, convex/system/ai/constants.ts
**Learned**: The effective flow detection is mostly heuristic from the last outbound message, not persisted session state; post-createRequest creates a new thread but does not delete the old one, while manual reset deletes thread history and ycloudMessages.

observations.title: Session summary: peretantico
observations.content:
## Goal
Investigate why the Tantico WhatsApp bot lets off-topic messages reach the LLM and why conversation context keeps growing.

## Instructions
User asked for analysis only, with exact code references, and explicitly did not want implementation proposals.

## Discoveries
- `isStronglyOffTopic` in `convex/ycloudBot.ts` only blocks a narrow set of math/programming/joke patterns; many unrelated intents still pass.
- `isOnTopicMessage` is very permissive: greetings, `ayuda`, `correo`, `direccion`, address tokens, and any message with 6+ digits are treated as on-topic.
- `shouldBlockMessage` bypasses topic filtering entirely when `hasActiveFlow` or `applicantNeedsProfile` is true, except for the narrow strong-off-topic regexes.
- `botSessions` do not really track flow progress: `serviceId`, `fieldIds`, and `currentFieldIndex` are only reset/cleared and were not found being populated anywhere in the codebase.
- The LLM context comes from the persistent agent `threadId`, not from `ycloudMessages`; `tanticoAgent.generateText` reuses the same thread with no pruning/windowing.
- `createRequest` does create a new thread and resets the session to `INIT`, but it does not delete the old thread; manual `reset` is much more aggressive because it deletes thread history and `ycloudMessages` and clears profile association.
- There was no separate conversation log attached in the prompt, so examples had to be derived from code behavior rather than a supplied transcript.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Read and analyzed `convex/ycloudBot.ts`, including filter logic, reset flow, and agent invocation.
- ✅ Read `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` and identified prompt-scope and enforcement gaps.
- ✅ Read `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts` and confirmed post-request thread/session reset behavior.
- ✅ Read `convex/whatsappBotState.ts`, `convex/schema.ts`, `convex/ycloudState.ts`, and `convex/conversationState.ts` to trace session persistence and context lifecycle.

## Next Steps
- Provide the user with a precise gap analysis, prompt gaps, context-growth root cause, and affected files.

## Relevant Files
- convex/ycloudBot.ts — pre-LLM filtering, reset command, agent thread usage, context prompt construction
- convex/system/ai/constants.ts — Tantico system prompt and scope rules
- convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts — request creation and post-success thread/session reset
- convex/whatsappBotState.ts — applicant/session persistence and reset helpers
- convex/conversationState.ts — conversation/thread linkage per contact
- convex/ycloudState.ts — WhatsApp message persistence and manual reset cleanup
- convex/schema.ts — schema for botApplicants, botSessions, conversations, and ycloudMessages

observations.title: sdd/chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Chat Context Guard and Thread Reset

## Intent
Reduce WhatsApp scope leaks by tightening pre-LLM filtering, hardening the system prompt, and explicitly closing chat context after request creation so stale thread history stops influencing later turns.

## Scope
### In Scope
- Strengthen `convex/ycloudBot.ts` blocking rules for off-topic, profile, and active-flow paths.
- Harden `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` with explicit denial rules for general knowledge, math, weather, currency, email drafting, and redaction-style tasks.
- Add post-`createRequest` closure/rotation behavior with a user-facing completion notice.

### Out of Scope
- Changes outside the WhatsApp bot / Tantico flow.
- Broad AI policy redesign or multi-channel conversation lifecycle work.

## Approach
Tighten guards before the model runs: expand strong off-topic detection, narrow “on-topic” acceptance, and remove the current bypass where active flow / missing profile can suppress blocking. After successful `createRequest`, close the current conversation record, rotate to a fresh thread/session state, and require an explicit closure message that tells the user the request was created and the previous context was restarted for the next interaction.

## Affected Areas
| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `convex/ycloudBot.ts` | Modified | Stronger scope classification and blocking precedence before `generateText`. |
| `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` | Modified | Non-advisory prompt rules for unsupported requests and post-create closure wording. |
| `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts` | Modified | Return / trigger completion metadata for closure + rollover flow. |
| `convex/conversationState.ts` | Modified | Mark completed conversation closed while attaching the fresh thread to the next open context. |

## Risks
| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Valid short replies get blocked | Med | Keep allowlist for field answers, confirmations, IDs, emails, and addresses tied to active prompts. |
| Duplicate closure messaging | Med | Define a single owner for the completion message (tool result or post-tool handler). |
| Reset breaks status follow-ups | Low | Preserve request-status intents and allow new conversations to query REQ codes immediately. |

## Rollback Plan
Revert the guard changes in `ycloudBot.ts`, restore the prior prompt text, and disable completion closure so request creation returns to current thread reuse behavior.

## Dependencies
- Existing WhatsApp session/thread mutations in `conversationState` and `whatsappBotState`.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Off-topic prompts are blocked before LLM execution even during active flow/profile collection.
- [ ] Unsupported prompt categories no longer get answered by the assistant.
- [ ] After request creation, the user receives an explicit completion/closure message and the next turn uses fresh context.

observations.title: Session summary: peretantico
observations.content:
## Goal
Create the SDD proposal for `chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset` in `peretantico`.

## Instructions
- Automatic mode: no follow-up questions.
- Do not run build commands.
- Verify claims against code before asserting.
- Keep scope constrained to the WhatsApp service.
- Persist the proposal in Engram with topic key `sdd/chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset/proposal`.

## Discoveries
- `convex/ycloudBot.ts` currently blocks strong off-topic content first, but then bypasses blocking entirely when `hasActiveFlow` or `applicantNeedsProfile` is true.
- `isOnTopicMessage` is permissive enough to allow generic emails, addresses, digit-heavy inputs, and broad help phrases even outside a valid service flow.
- `createRequest.ts` already rotates to a new thread and resets session/conversation linkage, but `conversationState.resetConversationForContact` reopens the conversation and there is no explicit closure message requirement in the prompt.

observations.content:
# Delta for WhatsApp Service Scope and Conversation Lifecycle

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Pre-LLM Scope Guard
The system MUST classify inbound WhatsApp text before any LLM call. Strong off-topic detection MUST reject clearly unsupported intents, including generic math, programming, entertainment, weather, currency conversion, email drafting/redaction, general knowledge, and academic assistance. On-topic gating MUST continue allowing service intents, request-status intents, and valid short operational answers. Active service flow or missing-profile state MUST NOT bypass a strong off-topic rejection.
(Previously: active flow / profile collection could suppress blocking, and strong off-topic coverage was narrower.)

#### Scenario: Strong off-topic blocked during active flow
- GIVEN the user is in profile collection or field capture
- WHEN the inbound text asks for an unsupported non-service task
- THEN the system MUST block it before `generateText`
- AND it MUST send the deterministic fallback response

#### Scenario: Valid short answer preserved
- GIVEN the last outbound message asked for a service field or confirmation
- WHEN the user replies with "sí", "no", a numeric option, phone number, document number, address, or email
- THEN the system MUST allow the message to continue through normal service handling

#### Scenario: Media remains unblocked
- GIVEN the active field expects or may accept a file
- WHEN the user sends media with or without short caption text
- THEN the pre-LLM guard MUST preserve current media-handling behavior

### Requirement: Prompt Scope Enforcement
The assistant prompt MUST explicitly forbid answering unsupported non-service intents, including currency conversion, email drafting or redaction, weather, general knowledge, and generic academic help. For unsupported requests, the assistant MUST return exactly the approved fallback text and MUST NOT call tools for those intents.
(Previously: the prompt rejected some general topics, but unsupported categories and fallback wording were incomplete.)

#### Scenario: Unsupported request reaches prompt
- GIVEN a non-service request is not blocked earlier
- WHEN the model evaluates the prompt rules
- THEN it MUST refuse the request using the exact fallback text
- AND it MUST NOT produce alternative advisory content

### Requirement: Post-Request Context Closure
After a successful `createRequest`, the system MUST close the completed conversation context, rotate to a fresh thread/session context for the next interaction, and send one explicit informational message confirming that the request was created and that a new conversation context will be used for follow-up.
(Previously: request creation reset thread/session state without a guaranteed explicit closure notice and without preserving a closed prior context.)

#### Scenario: Successful request closes prior context
- GIVEN all required service data is confirmed and `createRequest` succeeds
- WHEN the success response is emitted to the user
- THEN the completed conversation record MUST be marked closed
- AND the next inbound turn MUST use a fresh thread/session context

#### Scenario: Single completion notice owner
- GIVEN request creation succeeds
- WHEN the user receives the completion response
- THEN exactly one closure/informational message MUST be sent
- AND it MUST tell the user the next step for future interactions

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Deterministic Fallback Reply
The unsupported-intent fallback MUST be a single canonical text shared by guard and prompt paths so off-topic handling is consistent regardless of where rejection occurs.

#### Scenario: Same fallback across layers
- GIVEN a blocked unsupported intent
- WHEN rejection happens in either the pre-LLM guard or prompt layer
- THEN the user MUST receive the same fallback wording

observations.title: sdd/chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset/design
observations.content:
# Design: Chat Context Guard and Thread Reset

## Technical Approach
Tighten scope enforcement before `tanticoAgent.generateText`, make the prompt refuse unsupported categories with a fixed fallback, and move post-request closure into an explicit completion contract so request creation both informs the user and rotates the conversation to a fresh thread. The design keeps changes localized to the current WhatsApp bot path and existing session/conversation mutations.

## Architecture Decisions
| Decision | Options | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Guard order in `convex/ycloudBot.ts` | Keep current bypasses; block only after flow checks; classify in staged predicates | Stage checks as: media/empty pass-through → strong off-topic hard block → active-flow/profile-aware allowlist check → weak off-topic block → LLM | Today `hasActiveFlow`/`applicantNeedsProfile` disable blocking entirely. Strong off-topic must win first; short service answers must still pass when the bot is collecting data. |
| Predicate boundaries | Broad on-topic keywords only; single boolean block; separate strong/off-topic/on-topic predicates | Split into `isStronglyOffTopic`, `isAllowedFlowReply`, and narrower `isOnTopicMessage` | Clear predicate ownership reduces false positives and makes precedence explicit. Strong off-topic covers math/general-knowledge/redaction-style asks; on-topic remains limited to services, profile fields, and REQ tracking. |
| Completion ownership | Tool emits user text; handler emits user text; both can emit | `createRequest` returns structured completion metadata; `ycloudBot.processInboundMessage` is the single sender | One owner prevents duplicate WhatsApp messages and keeps transport logging (`ycloudState`, `conversationState`) centralized in the existing outbound path. |
| Thread reset strategy | Reuse same thread and wipe history; rotate only session; create fresh thread and close old conversation | Create a fresh thread, mark prior conversation closed, patch session to new thread/state, then send closure message | Rotation is safer than partial deletion because old assistant context cannot leak into the next turn even if deletion is unavailable. |

## Data Flow
`Inbound WhatsApp` → `processInboundMessage`
→ normalize text + inspect last outbound/session
→ `strong off-topic?` yes → send fixed denial
→ no → `flow/profile reply allowed?` yes → continue
→ `on-topic?` no → send fixed denial
→ yes → `tanticoAgent.generateText`
→ `createRequest` (if confirmed)
→ returns `{ ok, applicationNumber, completion: { closeConversation, newThreadId, message } }`
→ `processInboundMessage` performs closure mutations if needed
→ sends one closure/confirmation message on the new open thread.

## File Changes
| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `convex/ycloudBot.ts` | Modify | Reorder filtering, add explicit predicate boundaries, consume structured completion metadata, and own final outbound closure send. |
| `convex/system/ai/constants.ts` | Modify | Add explicit deny categories and mandate a deterministic fallback sentence for unsupported requests and post-create behavior. |
| `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts` | Modify | Stop directly messaging; return completion payload after request creation and thread/session rollover succeeds or partially fails. |
| `convex/conversationState.ts` | Modify | Add/adjust mutation to close current conversation and attach the rotated thread to the next open context without reopening the closed record implicitly. |

## Interfaces / Contracts
```ts
type RequestCompletion = {
  closeConversation: boolean;
  message: string;
  newThreadId?: string;
  closureApplied: boolean;
};
```
`createRequest` should return `completion` only on successful request creation. `processInboundMessage` must send `completion.message` once, and only after checking a truthy completion contract.

## Testing Strategy
| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Unit | Predicate precedence and allowlists | Cover strong off-topic, active-flow short answers, profile-field replies, and REQ lookups in `ycloudBot.ts`. |
| Integration | Successful request creation rotates thread and session | Exercise `createRequest` + conversation/session mutations and assert old conversation closes, new thread is stored. |
| Integration | Partial closure failure | Simulate thread creation/patch failure and assert request still succeeds with `closureApplied=false` plus safe fallback text. |

## Migration / Rollout
No migration required. Existing contacts keep their current `botSessions`; the next inbound message will evaluate under the stricter guards. If a closure operation partially fails after request creation, preserve the created request as source of truth, return a confirmation that omits “context restarted” wording, and leave the current thread/session untouched for retry-safe recovery.

## Open Questions
- [ ] Whether `conversationState.ensureConversationForContact` must stop reopening `closed` rows, or whether closure should create a new row immediately.
- [ ] Whether old agent-thread deletion is still needed once fresh-thread rotation becomes the guarantee.

observations.title: Verified request reset keeps conversation open
observations.content:
**What**: Verified that the current WhatsApp request-completion path resets the conversation to a new thread and INIT session state, but reuses the same open conversation record and does not guarantee a dedicated closure notice.
**Why**: The spec for `chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset` needed evidence from current code before defining modified behavior.
**Where**: `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts`, `convex/conversationState.ts`, `convex/ycloudBot.ts`, `convex/system/ai/constants.ts`
**Learned**: `resetConversationForContact` currently patches the primary conversation back to `status: "open"`, so explicit close/rotate semantics must be specified rather than assumed from existing reset behavior.

observations.title: Identified implicit reopen in conversation state
observations.content:
**What**: Identified that `conversationState.ensureConversationForContact` and `setThreadIdForContact` automatically reopen conversations whose status is `closed`.
**Why**: This affects the design for post-request closure because a rotated conversation could be reopened on the next inbound turn unless closure semantics change.
**Where**: `convex/conversationState.ts`
**Learned**: Thread rotation alone is not enough; the conversation record lifecycle must also avoid implicit reopen of the just-closed row.

observations.title: Implemented chat context guard and post-request closure
observations.content:
**What**: Implementé endurecimiento de filtro de contexto del bot WhatsApp, hardening del prompt y cierre post-createRequest con mensaje explícito al usuario.
**Why**: El usuario reportó fuga de contexto/off-topic y crecimiento de tokens por conversaciones largas; pidió aplicar los 3 niveles completos.
**Where**: convex/ycloudBot.ts, convex/system/ai/constants.ts, convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts, convex/conversationState.ts, convex/system/ai/unsupportedIntent.ts
**Learned**: El flujo ahora centraliza el mensaje de rechazo fuera de alcance y el cierre post-request en la capa de transporte para evitar mensajes duplicados. La verificación quedó parcial por ausencia de test runner.

observations.title: Centralized fallback and completion notice ownership
observations.content:
**What**: Centralized unsupported-intent fallback text in a shared module and made `ycloudBot` the single owner of post-`createRequest` completion sending.
**Why**: Off-topic rejections needed deterministic wording across guard/prompt layers, and request success messaging was duplicating because both the model flow and transport path could emit final notices.
**Where**: convex/system/ai/unsupportedIntent.ts, convex/system/ai/constants.ts, convex/ycloudBot.ts, convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts
**Learned**: Suppressing duplicate completion notices is safer when tool output carries completion metadata and the transport decides whether to send the restart-aware or fallback-safe message.

observations.title: Preserved closed conversations during thread rotation
observations.content:
**What**: Changed conversation reset logic to close the active conversation row and create a fresh open row, while preserving closed history and deduplicating contact listings toward the open record.
**Why**: The new thread-rotation flow must prevent closed contexts from being reopened implicitly after request creation or manual reset.
**Where**: convex/conversationState.ts
**Learned**: Existing helpers assumed one conversation row per contact and deleted duplicates aggressively; preserving closure history requires every lookup/update path to prefer the open row instead of blindly reusing the newest row.

- Save the verify report to Engram under `sdd/chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset/verify-report`.
- Focus verification on pre-LLM guard hardening, prompt hardening, post-request closure behavior, and regression risk.

## Discoveries
- The repo currently has no detected test runner, no `test` script in `package.json`, and no relevant test files for this change, so none of the spec scenarios have runtime proof.
- The implementation is structurally aligned with the intended design: shared unsupported fallback, stronger pre-LLM guard order, transport-owned completion messaging, and fresh-thread conversation reset.
- `createRequest` sets `completion.closureApplied=true` before `ycloudBot` actually applies conversation closure/session rotation, so that flag overstates real closure success.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Retrieved and reviewed the proposal, spec, design, tasks, testing capabilities, and apply-progress artifacts from Engram.
- ✅ Inspected the touched implementation files: `convex/ycloudBot.ts`, `convex/system/ai/constants.ts`, `convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts`, `convex/conversationState.ts`, and `convex/system/ai/unsupportedIntent.ts`.
- ✅ Executed targeted ESLint on the touched Convex files successfully.
- ✅ Persisted the verification artifact to Engram at `sdd/chat-context-guard-and-thread-reset/verify-report`.
- 🔲 Missing targeted/unit/integration tests still need to be added before the change can be considered behaviorally verified.

## Next Steps
- Add targeted tests for pre-LLM guard precedence and valid active-flow replies.
- Add integration tests for successful request completion closure/rotation and single completion notice emission.
- Fix or clarify the `closureApplied` contract so it reflects actual closure application timing.

## Relevant Files
- convex/ycloudBot.ts — main WhatsApp inbound flow, guard ordering, and post-`createRequest` closure handling.
- convex/system/ai/constants.ts — prompt hardening and deterministic unsupported-intent refusal rules.
- convex/system/ai/tools/createRequest.ts — request creation plus completion metadata contract.
- convex/conversationState.ts — conversation closing and fresh-thread conversation creation.
- convex/system/ai/unsupportedIntent.ts — canonical shared fallback reply.

observations.title: Cached SDD automatic mode preference

**Why**: El usuario cambió de modo interactivo a automático para continuar fases sin pausas.
**Where**: Orquestación SDD de la sesión actual

observations.title: Extracted WhatsApp bot test helpers
observations.content:

**Why**: The project needed regression tests for the WhatsApp bot flow without changing runtime behavior or adding heavy Convex integration.
